mnemonics: JOHN H.A.S. GROUP BY & ORDER BY (JOIN, HAVING, AND, SELECT, GROUP BY, ORDER BY) - The order of operations for a complex query.
keyPoints: JOIN clauses (INNER, LEFT, RIGHT, FULL) are used to combine rows from two or more tables based on a related column.
The HAVING clause filters groups created by GROUP BY, whereas the WHERE clause filters individual rows.
Subqueries can be used in SELECT, FROM, and WHERE clauses to break down a complex problem into manageable parts.
commonMistakes: Using WHERE instead of HAVING to filter aggregated data after a GROUP BY operation.
Forgetting that columns in the SELECT list that are not aggregate functions must be included in the GROUP BY clause.
Creating ambiguous column references in JOIN queries by not prefixing column names with the table alias or name.
